chunks.text: Raluca Ada Popa is an associate professor of computer science at UC Berkeley. She is interested in security, systems, and applied cryptography. Raluca developed practical systems that protect data confidentiality by computing over encrypted data, as well as designed new encryption schemes that underlie these systems. Some of her systems have been adopted into or inspired systems such as SEEED of SAP AG, Microsoft SQL Server's Always Encrypted Service, and others.
chunks.question: What are Raluca Popa's main research interests?

chunks.text: Raluca received her PhD in computer science as well as her two BS degrees, in computer science and in mathematics, from MIT. She is the recipient of an Intel Early Career Faculty Honor award, George M. Sprowls Award for best MIT CS doctoral thesis, a Google PhD Fellowship, a Johnson award for best CS Masters of Engineering thesis from MIT, and a CRA Outstanding undergraduate award from the ACM.
chunks.question: Where did Raluca Popa earn all her degrees?

chunks.text: Education: 2014, Doctor of Philosophy, Computer Science, Massachusetts Institute of Technology. 2010, Masters of Engineering, Computer Science, Massachusetts Institute of Technology. 2009, Bachelor's degree, Computer Science and Mathematics, Massachusetts Institute of Technology.
chunks.question: When did Raluca Popa earn her PhD?

chunks.text: Research Areas: Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT), Security (SEC). Research Centers: Berkeley Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence (RDI), Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD), Sky Computing Lab (Sky).
chunks.question: What are Raluca Popa's research areas?

chunks.text: Selected Honors and Awards: Grace Murray Hopper Award (2021), NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) (2020), Jim and Donna Gray Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching of Computer Science (2020), MIT Tech Review Top 35 Innovators Under 35 (TR35) (2019), Bakar Fellows Spark Award (2019), Microsoft New Faculty Fellow (2019), Okawa Research Grant (2019), Hellman Fellow (2018), Sloan Research Fellow (2018).
chunks.question: What prestigious award did Raluca Popa receive in 2021?
